title: Playbook 'off mode writes no retention state' contradicts baseline-delete behavior

findingDetails.evidence: Doc says 'Off mode is inert' / 'Off mode writes no audit or retention state' and step 3 says 'capture zero retention mutations'; but the off-mode regression test asserts swept:1, deletedIds:[1] (memory-retention-feedback-learning.vitest.ts:109) — baseline TTL deletion still runs when the master flag is off.
findingDetails.why: 'Inert' is true only for the feedback reducer, not the whole sweep. An operator seeding expired rows and following step 3 verbatim would observe baseline deletion and could record a false test failure.
findingDetails.recommendation: Reword to 'Off mode runs no feedback-driven audit or retention decisions; the baseline TTL sweep still deletes expired non-protected rows' and adjust step 3 to expect baseline deletion.
